text.title: Service Mesh Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to apply Service Mesh end-to-end. Each step has named input, action, and output. Decision gates reference rules from `01-core-rules.xml`.

step.name: select-mesh
step.input: Service count, latency SLO, multi-cluster need, eBPF availability
step.action: Apply the mesh-selection decision matrix: mTLS-only → Istio Ambient. Advanced L7 (mirroring, fault injection) → Istio or Linkerd. Resource-constrained → Linkerd or Ambient. Multi-cluster → Istio or Cilium. Network-heavy + eBPF expertise → Cilium.
step.output: Chosen mesh + rationale recorded as ADR

step.name: bootstrap-mtls-permissive
step.action: Install the mesh control plane; deploy the data plane (sidecar or ztunnel/waypoint) across the chosen namespaces; configure mTLS in permissive mode; verify certificate issuance via mesh telemetry.
step.output: Mesh control + data plane live, mTLS in permissive mode

step.name: validate-coverage
step.action: Run for ≥7 days; verify every service-to-service path is encrypted in telemetry; document exceptions (external services, legacy apps) before promoting to strict.

step.name: promote-to-strict-namespace-by-namespace
step.action: Set PeerAuthentication to STRICT one namespace at a time; monitor for 4xx/5xx spikes; roll back the namespace within 5 minutes if SLO breach detected.
step.output: Strict mTLS enforced per validated namespace

step.name: wire-canary-and-outlier-detection
step.action: Add VirtualService + DestinationRule per service: canary weight steps gated on SLO, outlier detection with 30s ejection. Commit to repo and apply via GitOps.
step.output: Traffic-management policies live, reviewable in git

section.title: Decision gates
section.p: Between steps, consult `content/06-decision-tree.xml` if the observed signal does not match expected output. The decision tree always terminates at a rule id from `01-core-rules.xml` or at `skip-this-methodology`.
